name: Monte de Vico Loop
name_original: Ermita San Marcos, Cueva de Vico, Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de Vico – circular
description: This moderate 3.8-mile (6.1 km) jogging loop takes you past the Hermitage San Marcos, Vico Cave, and Monastery of Our Lady of Vico.
region_intro: Jogging around Arnedo offers diverse terrain within the La Rioja region of Spain. The area is characterized by the Cidacos River valley, surrounded by mountains and unique rock formations, providing a variety of landscapes for running. Routes often follow the river, passing through tree-lined paths and extensive almond and olive groves, with views of colossal rock formations. The region also features prominent limestone formations like Peña Isasa and various hills, offering options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Arnedo The most popular jogging route is Monte de Vico Loop, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 48 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural trails around the Monasterio de Vico, offering scenic views and a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Arnedo, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route offers a relatively flat experience around the town, suitable for a steady run. Local runners also love the Ermita de San Marcos – Peña Isasa Summit loop from Arnedo, a 26.6 miles (42.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain including significant elevation gain towards Peña Isasa, often completed in about 6 hours. Jogging around Arnedo is defined by river valleys, surrounding mountains, and unique rock formations. The network of running routes offers options for both casual joggers and more serious trail runners. The routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Arnedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Arnedo?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Arnedo, offering diverse terrain for all fitness levels. More than 300 runners have already explored these paths using komoot, enjoying the natural beauty of the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails near Arnedo?
A: Jogging routes around Arnedo feature a variety of terrains. You'll find pleasant, tree-lined paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Cidacos River valley&lt;/strong&gt;, extensive almond and olive groves, and trails with views of colossal rock formations. More challenging options ascend prominent limestone formations like &lt;strong&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/strong&gt;, offering significant elevation changes and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Arnedo?
A: While many routes offer a moderate challenge, you can find relatively flat options suitable for a steady run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.2 km path that offers a gentle experience around the town.

Q: Are there challenging running routes for experienced trail runners near Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo offers several challenging routes for experienced trail runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos – Peña Isasa Summit loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.9 km trail with significant elevation gain, leading towards &lt;strong&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/strong&gt;. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Vico – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de Vico&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.7 km difficult route with substantial climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can run along the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, explore trails near the &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de Vico&lt;/strong&gt; and the intriguing &lt;strong&gt;Cueva de Vico&lt;/strong&gt;, or enjoy views of the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable point of interest along some paths.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes around Arnedo?
A: Yes, many of the running paths around Arnedo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990071404/monte-de-vico-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Vico Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural trails around the &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Vico&lt;/strong&gt;. For a longer, more challenging loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos – Cueva de Vico loop from Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de Vico&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine jogging with exploring historical sites in Arnedo?
A: Absolutely. Arnedo's rich history is intertwined with its natural landscape. You can jog past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a promontory overlooking the city, or explore trails that lead to the &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de Vico&lt;/strong&gt; and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any nearby towns or attractions accessible from Arnedo's running routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offers a beautiful section that connects Arnedo to the nearby village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;. Arnedillo is known for its picturesque rocky mountains and natural thermal pools, perfect for post-run relaxation.

Q: What is the best season for running in Arnedo?
A: Arnedo offers pleasant running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, offering a different, often serene, experience amidst the region's unique rock formations.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the jogging trails?
A: Yes, many routes offer excellent viewpoints. Trails leading up to &lt;strong&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/strong&gt; provide panoramic views across the Lower La Rioja region. The area around the &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de San Marcos&lt;/strong&gt; also offers scenic vistas, and natural features like &lt;strong&gt;Las Tres Tetas del Raposal&lt;/strong&gt; provide good vantage points with views of nearby towns.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the relatively flat sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, with its tree-lined paths and gentle gradients, could be suitable for families looking for an easier stroll or light jog. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Arnedo?
A: The running routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountain trails, and the unique natural features like the colossal rock formations and extensive groves. The variety ensures options for both casual joggers and serious trail runners.
tips: Hermitage of San Marcos, on the one hand the ruins of the old hermitage, and on the other the new hermitage restored in 2002.

Access to the cave, Monte Vico trail.
The trail is ideal for nature and outdoor lovers, and is suitable for people of different levels of hiking experience. Along the way, various species of flora and fauna can be observed, making the experience even more enriching.

Located outside the city walls, in the place where, according to tradition, the Virgin of Vico appeared to the Kan of Vico, head of one of the Arab Communities of Arnedo, in the year 1045. It was in 1456 when the Franciscan Fray López de Salinas founded this monastery that reached its greatest splendor in the 17th century.

faqs.question: What is the estimated jogging time for this circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed for jogging and typically takes around 48 minutes to complete. It covers a distance of approximately 6.1 km (3.8 miles).

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate and requires good physical condition. While most paths are accessible, some sections demand firm footing, making it less ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near the main entrance of the Monastery of Our Lady of Vico. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for trails starting at such landmarks to have nearby parking facilities.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, starting through an almond grove and leading into a diverse pine forest with Aleppo and stone pines. You'll traverse old terraces, pass an ancient masonry aqueduct, and ascend a ravine. Expect a mix of natural paths and some sections requiring firm footing.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the starting point at the Monastery of Our Lady of Vico, the Hermitage of San Marcos (both old ruins and a newer structure), and the Vico Cave. Don't miss the charming channeled spring of Fuente de San Marcos and the panoramic views from elevated points, including those of the Genil valley.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers fabulous panoramic views of the Genil valley and other picturesque vistas from various elevated points. The geological fault known as Falla de la Sierra del Camorro also contributes to the stunning scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Given the presence of almond groves and diverse forests, spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, so check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is this circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there is no specific information regarding dog-friendliness or leash requirements. It's always best to assume dogs should be kept on a leash and to practice Leave No Trace principles by cleaning up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access this circular jogging route.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will find the charming, channeled spring of Fuente de San Marcos along the route, which provides a potential water source. However, it's always advisable to carry your own water, especially for jogging.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with parts of the Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos, the Vía Verde del Cidacos, and the Via verde Cidacos.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Our Lady of Vico
